description_long: This is a tall, amphora-like vase with a smooth, matte beige surface and a distinctive circular handle that arches from one side of the neck to the other, forming an open loop above the main body. The body is bulbous and tapers gently toward the base, while the neck is elongated and cylindrical, flaring slightly at the rim. The handle is thick and robust, seamlessly integrated into the upper portion of the vase, giving it a sculptural and modern appearance. The surface is unadorned, with no visible patterns or decorative motifs, emphasizing the purity of its form and material. The vase appears to be made from ceramic or clay, with a subtle speckled texture that adds a natural, handcrafted feel. Its design suggests it would serve as a striking decorative piece in a contemporary urban environment, such as a public plaza or modern park.
description: A tall, matte beige vase with a bulbous body, elongated neck, and a thick circular handle arching over the top. Its smooth, unadorned surface and sculptural form give it a modern, minimalist appeal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent circular handle arching over the neck and the bulbous body below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated neck and the seamless integration of the handle into the vase's body.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the circular handle and flared rim clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the smooth, continuous curve of the neck and body.
